title: Govkit Extension Manifest
description: Self-describing manifest for a govkit extension pack. The extension lives in-place under <project>/extensions/<id>/ and is discovered by govkit at apply/validate time. All file paths inside this manifest are relative to the extension folder (<project>/extensions/<id>/) unless explicitly stated otherwise.

id.description: Lowercase kebab-case identifier. Must match the extension folder name.

paths.description: Extension-folder-relative paths to contract documents shipped by this extension.

applies_to.description: Glob patterns matched against feature-touched files to determine applicability.

capabilities.description: Capability tokens the agent can match against feature intent.

relates_to.description: Declares this contract set's relationship to project contracts, including core govkit contracts and contracts from other installed extensions. Paths in extends and supersedes are project-root-relative (not extension-folder-relative).

extends.description: Project-root-relative paths to contracts this set layers additional constraints on top of.

supersedes.description: Project-root-relative paths to contracts this set replaces. Requires an ADR in the consuming project.

path.description: Extension-folder-relative path to a reusable governance template.

implementation_profiles.description: Product-naming profiles that map contract sets to concrete default product bindings the consuming team may customize. Unlike contract_sets, a profile is allowed to name products (LangGraph, LiteLLM, …) and refine a contract; it is advisory and the neutral contract remains authoritative on conflict. Profiles are exempt from the undeclared-overlap heuristic.

path.description: Extension-folder-relative path to the implementation-profile document (defaults the team can customize).

profiles_for.description: contract_set ids this profile supplies default product bindings for.

authority.description: 'defaults' — the profile is advisory; the neutral contract wins on any conflict.

product_selection_requires_adr.description: When true, deviating from the profile's default product bindings requires an ADR in the consuming project.

gate.description: Reserved for a future blocking conformance gate. Advisory-only today; no CI gate is wired to profiles.

agent_guidance.description: Named guidance blocks read by agent skills. Keys are phase names (e.g. architecture_preflight, implementation_plan, validation); values are arrays of instruction strings.
